zh.Skenfrith, Monmouthshire, Wales: 威爾士蒙姆爾斯肯福利特
zh.lifeSummary: Sarah Cocke於1385年出生在英國。她和William Nathan Cox至少有1個兒子。她去世於1433年在英國波克夏享年48歲。

zh-hans.Skenfrith, Monmouthshire, Wales: 威尔士蒙姆爾斯肯福利特
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sarah Cocke于1385年出生在英国。她和William Nathan Cox至少有1个儿子。她去世于1433年在英国波克夏享年48岁。

nameMeanings.name: Cocke
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English:
nickname from Middle English cok (Old English and Old French coc) ‘rooster’.
patronymic from the Old French and Middle English personal name Cok, of uncertain origin but perhaps derived from the word in 1 above.

variants.name: Alcock
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Alcok, a pet form of any of the many medieval personal names beginning with Al-, especially Alan and Alexander. The Middle English hypocoristic suffix -cok (see Cocke ) was very commonly added to personal names in Middle English: compare for example Hancock and Wilcock .

variants.name: Cockman
meanings: English: occupational name, from either Cocke + man ‘Cock's servant’, or else from Middle English cok(e) + man ‘cook's assistant or servant’.

variants.name: Cook
meanings: English: occupational name for a cook, a seller of cooked meats, or a keeper of an eating house, from Middle English cok, coke, cook, couk, cuk(e) (Old English cōc) ‘cook’ or ‘seller of cooked foods’. See also Kew .
Irish and Scottish: usually identical in origin with the English name (see 1 above), but in some cases a shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Cúg ‘son of Hugo’ (see McCook ).

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames meaning ‘cook’, such as German and Jewish Koch , Dutch Kook , Polish Kucharz and Kucharczyk , Slovenian and Croatian Kuhar , North German Kuk .

variants.name: Cockrell
meanings: English: nickname from Anglo-Norman French, Middle English cokerel ‘young cock’ (see Cocke ).

variants.name: Cocklin
meanings: English: from an Old French and Middle English personal name Cokelin, a French double diminutive of Cocke 2.
Perhaps also an Americanized form of Swiss German and South German Köchlin, Koechlin, or Köchling, all diminutives of Koch .

variants.name: Cox
meanings: English: variant of Cocke and Cook , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.
Irish (Ulster): mistranslation of Mac Con Coille (‘son of Cú Choille’, a personal name meaning ‘hound of the wood’), as if formed with coileach ‘cock, rooster’.
Dutch and Flemish: genitivized patronymic from the personal name Cock, a vernacular short form of Cornelius .

variants.name: Cocking
meanings: English:
habitational name from Cocking in Sussex. The placename denoted ‘(the settlement of) Cocking's people’, from the Old English personal name Cocca + the group-name suffix -ingas.
patronymic from either of two Middle English personal names, Cok(k)ing (Old English Coccing) or Cokin (probably Old French in origin), both of which are pet forms of Cocke 2.

variants.name: Bulcock
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English bule, bole ‘bull’ + the diminutive suffix -cock (see Cocke ). Compare Bocock .

variants.name: Coxon
meanings: English: patronymic from Cock (an affectionate term of address; see Cocke ) or the surname Cox , with the addition of son ‘son’.

nameMeanings.name: Sarah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac. According to the Book of Genesis, she was originally called Sarai (possibly meaning ‘contentious’ in Hebrew), but had her name changed by God to the more auspicious Sarah ‘princess’ in token of a greater blessing (Genesis 17:15, ‘And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be’). This has been one of the most enduringly popular girls' names.

variants.name: Sara
meanings: Variant of   Sarah .  This is the form used in the Greek of the New Testament (Hebrews 11:11).

en.lifeSummary: Sarah Cocke was born in 1385, in England. She had at least 1 son with William Nathan Cox. She died in 1433, in Berkshire, England, at the age of 48.

fr.lifeSummary: Sarah Cocke est née en 1385, à Angleterre. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec William Nathan Cox. Elle est décédée en 1433, à Berkshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 48 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Sarah Cocke wurde 1385 in England geboren. Sie hatte mit William Nathan Cox mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1433 im Alter von 48 Jahren in Berkshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Sarah Cocke è nata il nel 1385 nel England. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con William Nathan Cox. Morì nel 1433, nel Berkshire, England, all'età di 48 anni.

ja.Berkshire, England: イギリスバークシュアイア

ja.lifeSummary: Sarah Cockeは1385年にイギリスで生まれた。彼女はWilliam Nathan Coxとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1433年にイギリスバークシュアイアで48歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sarah Cocke(은)는 1385년 영국에서 태어났다. 그녀는 William Nathan Cox(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1433년 영국 버크실르에서 48세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sarah Cocke nasceu em 1385, em Inglaterra. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com William Nathan Cox. Ela faleceu em 1433, em Berkshire, Inglaterra, com 48 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Sarah Cocke родилась в 1385 г. в местности Англия. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от William Nathan Cox. Она умерла в 1433 г. в местности биркшир, Англия в возрасте 48 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Sarah Cocke nació en 1385, en Inglaterra. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con William Nathan Cox. Murió en 1433, en Berkshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 48 años.
